name: SM2135 LED driver
description: The SM2135 component represents a SM2135 LED driver chain ([SM2135 description](https://github.com/arendst/Sonoff-Tasmota/files/3656603/SM2135E_zh-CN_en-US_translated.pdf), [SM2135 description](https://github.com/arendst/Sonoff-Tasmota/files/3656603/SM2135E_zh-CN_en-US_translated.pdf)) in ESPHome. Communication is done with two GPIO pins (MOSI and SCLK). It is used in some smart light bulbs:

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed â€” e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed â€” e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: Cw Current
config_entries.description: The current used for the white channel. Defaults to `10mA`. Can be one of `10mA`, `15mA`, `20mA`, `25mA`, `30mA`, `35mA`, `40mA`, `45mA`, `50mA`, `55mA`, `60mA`.

config_entries.label: RGB Current
config_entries.description: The current used for the RGB channel. Defaults to `20mA`. Can be one of `10mA`, `15mA`, `20mA`, `25mA`, `30mA`, `35mA`, `40mA`, `45mA`.

config_entries.label: Separate Modes
config_entries.description: Use separate RGB/CW modes instead of writing all 5 values as RGB. Defaults to `true`, keep it at `true` if your SM2135 chip variant does not support simultaneous CW and RGB modes (e.g. SM2135E). Set this to `false` when your SM2135 chip variant supports having CW and RGB leds on at the same time (e.g. SM2135EH/SM2135EJ).
